steps.title: Keep subject and verb joined
steps.content: The sentence's subject is "the strings inside the instrument," and its verb phrase is "are plucked not struck." Standard English does not put punctuation between a subject and its verb.

steps.title: Reject sentence breaks
steps.content: Choice A puts a colon inside the clause. Choice C adds an unnecessary dash between subject and verb. Choice D ends the sentence before the verb phrase arrives. The answer is B.
